title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 19th March 2026 | TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Bought himself today on cheap valuation
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said he bought HDFC Bank today, around Rs 795 (it came twice), purely on valuation - a rare ~15.4 PE and price-to-book of ~2.6. He noted management is confident, with interim chairman KK Mistry and MD Sashidhar Jagdishan giving positive assurances after the boardroom resignation.
He said Laurus Labs is definitely a buy in this correction, with results that keep appearing better and better. He grouped it with Natco as a buy.

Kutumba Rao said both Laurus and Natco can be bought. Natco has fully turned around and now pays a dividend (a token 10% interim dividend was approved). A board meeting on the 24th will separate/split the agrochemicals business. He is trying to meet management again and believes something else is cooking in the company.
Asked by the anchor whether Bank of India can be bought, Kutumba Rao said Bank of India and Manorama are both good working companies.

He called Manorama a good working company in a niche segment (e.g. making butter from nuts), with a QIP underway now. He recalled the last QIP preceded a ~200% return, and this QIP comes after a long time.
Kutumba Rao said Precision Wires can definitely be accumulated at true levels and advised starting to accumulate.

recommendations.note: Hold; exit on relief rally after Q4
recommendations.detail: For a caller down 25% on LTTS, he said 25% is not a big loss in current conditions. The whole IT-services sector is in a transformation stage so it is hard to call, but he advised holding because management backing gives flexibility. One could exit on a relief rally after Q4; review again in a few days.
